To what extent was entering the streaming service industry a strategically
good decision for Disney?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with a comprehensive introduction that situates Disneyâs strategic motivations and industry context
Research question is clearly stated, sharply focused and consistently linked to all analytical sections
A broad and appropriate range of analytical tools and sources is selected, demonstrating informed methodology choices
weaknesses: Minor lapses in expression and overly complex sentences slightly affect clarity
The methodological justification for each tool could be more explicitly tied back to the research question
Occasional scope creep (e.g., digressive competitor descriptions) slightly dilutes focus
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates top-band performance in two of three strands (topic communication and research question) and strong performance in methodology. Although small stylistic and linkage issues prevent it from being flawless, the overall clarity, coherence and purposeful method selection justify awarding the maximum 6 marks for Focus and Method.

strengths: Sources are generally relevant and applied to support key points in Porterâs analysis, financial ratios and benchmarking
Subject knowledge of business management concepts is clear and coherent, with mostly accurate application
Terminology and concepts are used correctly and consistently throughout the essay
weaknesses: Some data are approximate or drawn from less authoritative blogs and opinion pieces
Citation style is uneven and some claims lack proper referencing or unit standardization
Occasional oversimplifications (e.g., IP ownership equated directly with cost-free content) reduce analytical precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student exhibits solid subject knowledge and largely appropriate application of source material, meriting the upper half of the 3â4 band. However, gaps in source reliability and citation precision constrain the work from reaching the top band, so 4 marks are appropriate.

strengths: The essay presents a balanced, reasoned discussion with a clear overall judgment on Disney+âs strategic merit
Research draws on a good breadth of secondary data that are directly relevant to the research question
Analysis in Porterâs Five Forces, financial ratios and benchmarking is clearly articulated and supported with evidence
weaknesses: Critical evaluation remains uneven, with many limitations acknowledged but not rigorously probed
Assumptions (e.g., estimated capital employed, unquantified cost barriers) are not fully tested or justified
Counter-arguments and alternative strategic scenarios are mentioned but not explored in depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Performance clearly falls in the 4â6 band across all three strands of Critical Thinking. While the analysis is competent and conclusions are supported, the lack of deep, sophisticated critique and quantitative evaluation limits the work from higher bands. Awarding the top of the mid-band (6 marks) fairly reflects its strengths and weaknesses.

strengths: The report is very well structured, with all required EE sections present and logically ordered
Layout and formatting (word count, page numbers, table of contents, headings) meet IB conventions
Visual elements and tables are neatly integrated and labelled
weaknesses: Minor formatting inconsistencies in headings and citation layout
A few tables and charts lack complete labels or legends
Dense paragraphs could be broken up for improved readability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation fulfills all IB requirements with only minor lapses, clearly meeting the highest band descriptor. Consequently, it merits the full 4 marks for Presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Research question is explicit, sharply focused and integral to the essay.** The question âTo what extent was entering the streaming service industry a strategically good decision for Disney?â is concise, evaluative and sits at the heart of every subsequent section (Porterâs Five Forces, financial ratios, benchmarking, conclusions).

**Sustained alignment but some scope creep.** The methodology promises three tools that do connect to the RQ; however, occasional digressions (e.g., lengthy descriptions of Netflix features) dilute focus slightly. Overall, the formulation and linkage to discussion remain clear and purposeful, justifying a high-band mark rather than the maximum.

**Reasoned discussion culminating in a clear overall judgement.** The essay weighs positives (brand leverage, IP control, bundling) against challenges (profitability, rivalry, substitutes) and offers a balanced if cautious conclusion. Limitations of data and methodology are occasionally acknowledged.

**Evaluation remains partially critical.** Depth of critique is uneven; financial risks, implementation issues and alternative strategies are touched on but not probed rigorously. Recommendations are general rather than analytically derived. The work thus fits the 4-6 band but does not achieve the comprehensive critical evaluation expected for the top two levels.

**Clear and generally coherent understanding of Business Management content.** Concepts such as economies of scale, bargaining power, ARPU, ROCE, churn and benchmarking are explained accurately and applied to Disneyâs context. The narrative demonstrates a solid grasp of strategic analysis tools.

**Some unevenness in depth and precision.** Occasional oversimplifications (e.g., equating IP ownership directly with cost-free content) and limited treatment of strategic risk reduce authority. Nonetheless, knowledge remains more than adequate and is usually supported by sources, justifying the upper end of the 3-4 band.

**Terminology is largely accurate and used consistently.** Key termsââvertical integration,â âchurn rate,â âbundling,â âreturn on capital employed,â âeconomies of scaleâ and all five Porter forcesâare applied correctly throughout. This supports clarity of argument.

**Minor slips and occasional informality.** A few phrases are colloquial (âface of streaming,â âforces viewers to stay subscribedâ) and citation style is uneven, but these issues do not seriously undermine conceptual precision. The overall use of vocabulary aligns with the 3-4 bandâs descriptor of âmostly accurateâ, meriting its upper score.

**Clear analytical narrative with supporting evidence.** The Porterâs assessment links each force to Disneyâs specific circumstances; financial ratios are interpreted; benchmarking tables compare KPIs and draw reasoned comments. These demonstrate more than basic description.

**Depth and sophistication are moderate rather than advanced.** Several conclusions rest on untested assumptions (e.g., estimating capital employed, equating IP with immediate cost savings). Counter-arguments appear but are not fully explored, so analysis, while competent, lacks the nuanced insight and rigour of the 7-9 band. A solid mid-band score is warranted.

**Mostly relevant application of sources.** Data from annual reports, Statista charts and industry articles are woven into Porter, financial and benchmarking sections, demonstrating the ability to draw on evidence to support points. Citations are generally correct and a bibliography is provided.

**Gaps in appropriateness and accuracy temper effectiveness.** Some figures are approximate (e.g., guessed capital employed), a few sources are opinion pieces or blogs, and key data (e.g., profitability comparisons) are not always critically verified. These weaknesses place the work in the middle band rather than the highest.

**Reasonably appropriate range of methods and sources.** The student selects three recognised analytical tools (Porterâs Five Forces, financial/ratio analysis, competitive benchmarking) and draws data from company reports, Statista, Forbes, and other business media. Citations are provided and a Works Cited list is present.

**Evidence of informed but not fully âeffectiveâ selection.** Primary research is absent, reliance on secondary popular-press articles/blogs is high, and some calculations use rough estimates (e.g., capital employed). The range is adequate and generally relevant, warranting the middle bandâs upper score, but falls short of the consistently rigorous, well-justified sourcing required for the top band.

**Good breadth and clear relevance of research.** The essay employs corporate reports, industry databases (Statista), financial statements, and trade publications to address the RQ systematically. Evidence is up-to-date (2023â2024) and directly tied to streaming strategy.

**Some methodological constraints.** Absence of primary data and heavy reliance on secondary commentary limit the research from reaching an âexcellentâ (7-9) level, yet the material collected is sufficiently wide-ranging and pertinent to justify the 4-6 band, with quality leaning toward its higher end.

**Layout and formal elements are largely compliant.** Word count, candidate details, title page, page numbers, table of contents, section headings and consistent font usage are all present. Visuals (logo, chart) are neatly inserted and labelled.

**Minor inconsistencies but overall correct.** Some citation formatting is uneven and the table of contents page numbers may shift if edited, yet the essential IB formatting expectations are met. These small issues do not detract from an otherwise correct application, warranting the top band score.

**Clarity and completeness of topic communication are strong.** The introduction clearly establishes the focus on Disneyâs decision to launch Disney+ and succinctly outlines the historical context, industry background and strategic motives (declining cable viewership, control over IP, BAMTech acquisition). The reader is left in no doubt about what the essay will examine and why the issue is business-relevant.

**Minor limitations prevent full marks.** Although comprehensive, portions of the opening section contain small lapses in expression (e.g., imprecise parenthetical citations, some overly long sentences) and the central evaluative dimension (âto what extentâ) could be foregrounded more consistently. These blemishes keep the work just below an unequivocally âcomprehensiveâ standard, but the overall communication quality merits the upper band.

**Structure is logically organised and meets EE conventions.** The report contains title page, abstract information (word count, session), table of contents with page references, introduction, methodology, analysis sections, conclusion and full bibliography. Headings and sub-headings guide the reader effectively.

**Minor structural issues only.** Some sections (e.g., Financial Analysis) include incomplete tables and occasional formatting inconsistencies, but overall the arrangement facilitates coherent reading and satisfies all formal requirements, justifying the highest band.

annotations.comment: The âLimitationsâ section is descriptive; deepen your critique by analyzing how competitor platforms quantitatively affect Disney+ subscriber trends.
Assertion that social media and gaming are substitutes is unreferenced; cite industry reports or data to substantiate these substitution effects.
The Table of Contents is complete with accurate page references and a logical hierarchy, supporting ease of navigation and overall structure.
Parenthetical citations (e.g., â(cnbc)â) are imprecise; adopt a consistent in-text citation style (author, date) to enhance scholarly rigor.
Tool descriptions in the Methodology list their purposes but do not justify why each is most suitable for answering your research question; clarify this linkage.
Churn rate figures are stated without explanation of data source or calculation method; include methodology to validate these percentages.
Bundling strategy claims would be strengthened by quantitative uptake data (e.g., percentage of subscribers using bundles); consider adding these figures.
The research question is embedded in prose rather than highlighted as a central thesis; format it distinctly (e.g., as a heading) to foreground its importance.
Claims about high entry costs are unquantified; include specific cost figures or authoritative estimates to substantiate this barrier analysis.
Citation â(disney)â is unclear; replace with a full formal citation (Disney, year) to enhance source credibility and traceability.
Discussion of in-house streaming infrastructure lacks supporting evidence; provide data or references on Bamtechâs capabilities to validate this point.
The inclusion of an accurate Word Count at the beginning meets IB formatting requirements and demonstrates attention to formal report conventions.
Multiple unformatted hyperlinks disrupt flow; consolidate into a formal reference list with proper line breaks and hanging indent.
A placeholder author â---.â is used for repeated entries; replace with proper author notation as per style guidelines for clarity.
The clear and concise title effectively communicates the focus of the investigation, fulfilling the requirement to state the title at the start of the report.
This introductory paragraph is overly long and complex; consider breaking it into shorter sentences to improve clarity and reader engagement.
The âBargaining power of suppliersâ heading uses an inconsistent HTML structure; standardize heading levels (e.g., h3) for clarity and readability.
Phrasing like âforces viewers to stay subscribedâ is colloquial; replace with formal terms (e.g., switching costs) to align with Business Management terminology.
The âThreat of substitutes: Highâ heading should match the formatting and hierarchy of other Porterâs force headings for consistency.
Piracy is introduced descriptively but lacks analysis of scale; estimate revenue losses or cite piracy statistics to support your evaluation.
The âLimitationsâ subsection appears in-line rather than as a styled header; format consistently and expand on key strategic implications.
The pie chart lacks clear percentage labels and a legend; add direct annotations or a legend for each segment to improve interpretability.
Profit figures lack currency symbols and consistent formatting (e.g., show â$2.496 billion); apply standardized numeric conventions.
The description of subscription tiers is superficial; include details on tier pricing and feature differentiation to deepen analysis.
The âGlobal Presenceâ row in this table is incomplete; finish the sentence and provide the corresponding context or data.
Content spend figures require citation; include source (Statista, annual reports) and year to validate these financial data.
The Works Cited list has inconsistent punctuation and missing numbering; apply a uniform citation style throughout for professional presentation.
The âWorks Citedâ header formatting differs from other section headers; standardize font size and style for consistency.
URLs are embedded mid-sentence; move links to reference entries or footnotes to maintain narrative readability and citation clarity.
The Prime Video analysis diverges into multi-service benefits; refocus discussion strictly on how Disney+âs entry decision compares to competitor strategies.
The âContent warsâ section is largely descriptive; introduce comparative metrics (e.g., exclusive titles count) to critically evaluate Disneyâs USP.
The financial table contains placeholders (âThe amount thatâ); complete all entries to ensure clarity and coherence of data presentation.
Narrative reference âInitial investment... comment on thatâ appears as a draft note; replace with finalized analysis linking figures to your evaluation.
You state Q3 2024 profit of $47 million but earlier mention break-even in Q2; reconcile these figures or explain the reporting period differences.
Comments in the benchmarking table are truncated (e.g., churn rate row ends abruptly); ensure complete commentary for accurate reader interpretation.
Data and narrative are merged in the table; separate raw figures from analysis to enhance clarity and ease of reading.
Verify revenue figures and unit consistency (e.g., â19.8 billionâ vs â19.8 (DTC)â); standardize units for accurate financial comparison.
The benchmarking summary reiterates points without new insight; incorporate additional KPIs (e.g., user engagement) for deeper evaluation.
The conclusion restates analysis but lacks examination of alternative strategic paths; consider adding counterfactual scenarios to strengthen critical evaluation.
The ROCE calculation is based on an estimated capital employed without a sensitivity range; justify this estimate or include a range to reflect uncertainty.
The final paragraph synthesizes findings but omits actionable strategic recommendations; add clear next-step advice based on your analysis.
This dense paragraph would be clearer if divided into shorter paragraphs aligned to distinct thematic points.
Web citations lack access dates where required; include retrieval dates for online sources to meet formal referencing standards.
